specific_location: South Platte Park on bike trail about 500 yards south of Mineral Ave.
description: Stout bill; slender, long neck as opposed to juvenile Black-crowned's stouter appearance. Narrow streaks on breast and neck as opposed to BCNH's broad, blurry streaks. Tiny white spots and thin white edges on wings unlike BCNH's larger white spots.   Overall appearance was much more delicate than the juvenile BCNHs which I have photographed much more frequently along this same area of the S. Platte River/S. Platte Park.Behaviors: The bird was well-hidden behind shrubs bordering the bike trail. I watched it for several minutes during which it did not move but stood perfectly erect and still as shown in photos.Call: SilentPlumage: 1st Summer Juvenile
